_note: This is the seed template for .squad/roster.json. The single role below shows the schema — DELETE THIS ROLE when squad-role generates your real roles. The _note field is documentation only and is dropped on the next write by squad-roster. Fields prefixed with _ are reserved for documentation and ignored by the squad-roster skill.
_mode_note: `mode` mirrors .squad/goal.md (authoritative). squad-roster re-derives it from goal.md on every write; squad-spawn reads mode from goal.md, never from here.
_environment_note: The `environment` block is OPTIONAL and is the role's sandbox spec, materialized by skills/squad-env/scripts/provision.sh. `workspace` is the sandbox root (must be project-relative, no '..', and MUST be covered by file_scope so in-sandbox Edit/Write auto-approve). `dirs` are scaffolded subdirs. `env` is written to <workspace>/env and SOURCED (never exported globally). `context` seeds local reference material (kind copy|link run locally; kind fetch is a global_need). `tools` declare readiness: kind 'local' with an `install` command is installed INTO the sandbox; kind 'system'/'mcp' is never run — it is proposed to the user as a global_need.

roles._comment: EXAMPLE ROLE — your roles get generated here by /cheeky-squad-os:squad-role. Delete this entry (or wait — squad-role overwrites the file when it adds the first real role). This example is for schema reference only; active is false so squad-spawn will skip it.
roles.name: example-role-delete-me
roles.purpose: One sentence describing what this role does. Action-first. Specific artifact.

environment._comment: OPTIONAL sandbox spec. Omit the whole block for a role that needs no provisioned environment. The workspace glob below is also added to file_scope so the role's in-sandbox Edit/Write auto-approve.
